task_completion.description: The ENIAC QR code image is saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file icon named 'eniac_qr.png' visible on the Desktop or within a file manager window viewing the Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: The Grace Hopper QR code image is saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file icon named 'hopper_qr.png' visible on the Desktop or within a file manager window viewing the Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: The Transistor QR code image is saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file icon named 'transistor_qr.png' visible on the Desktop or within a file manager window viewing the Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: The QR manifest text file is created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file icon named 'qr_manifest.txt' visible on the Desktop or within a file manager window viewing the Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: The manifest file contains the correct mapping for ENIAC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor (like gedit or nano) or terminal output (like cat) showing the contents of qr_manifest.txt. It must include 'eniac_qr.png' and 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ENIAC'.

task_completion.description: The manifest file contains the correct mapping for Grace Hopper.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor or terminal output showing the contents of qr_manifest.txt. It must include 'hopper_qr.png' and 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Hopper'.

task_completion.description: The manifest file contains the correct mapping for Transistor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor or terminal output showing the contents of qr_manifest.txt. It must include 'transistor_qr.png' and 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transistor'.

integrity.description: The agent used a genuine method to generate the QR codes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web-based QR code generator open in the browser, a command-line tool (like 'qrencode') in the terminal, or a Python script being executed to create the images. The agent should not just be renaming random existing images.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Wikipedia URLs for the generation process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the inputs provided to the QR code generator (browser input field, terminal command arguments, or script variables). They should match the exact Wikipedia URLs, not placeholders.

integrity.description: The agent actively wrote the manifest file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor interface (GUI or CLI) or terminal redirection commands (e.g., echo '...' > qr_manifest.txt) demonstrating the agent constructing the file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires generating QR codes for three specific Wikipedia URLs: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ENIAC, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Hopper, and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transistor. The resulting images must be saved to the Desktop as eniac_qr.png, hopper_qr.png, and transistor_qr.png. A text file named qr_manifest.txt must also be created on the Desktop, containing the exact mapping between these three filenames and their respective URLs.
